title: Online Stint Phase-Change Features For Cliff Detection
summary: Teams react when tyres move from stable to cliff behavior, not just when absolute tyre age is high.
rationale: Teams react when tyres move from stable to cliff behavior, not just when absolute tyre age is high. Online change-point methods are built to detect persistent shifts from past observations only, which fits lap-by-lap degradation and lap-time delta sequences. This is a different signal than the current fixed age/degradation ratios.
implementation_hint: Within each sorted driver-race stint, compute causal change signals from `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation` increments, and position loss: rolling slope, rolling slope acceleration, residual-to-rolling-median, and simple Bayesian/CUSUM-style change scores. Add 'laps since last detected regime shift' and interact it with compound and laps remaining.
expected_effect: Can surface sudden pit urgency on tracks or stints where degradation accelerates earlier or later than the handcrafted expected-life thresholds.
risk: Noisy laps from traffic or yellow-flag effects can trigger false cliffs; robustify with median-based windows and require persistence across 2-3 laps.
